title: Strict Typing, Nullability, and Boundary Validation · Signal Triage
summary: Establish a strict type baseline without confusing static types with runtime validation. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a strict type and boundary contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations.
Model null, missing, unknown, and unchecked indexed access explicitly.
Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:

# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-typing-migration-1whs6w: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Typing Migration into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-typing-migration-1whs6w:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Typing Migration applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Ratchet strictness without permanently exempting legacy areas.
